name: Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular by the Río Guadiana
name_original: Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana a Las Villuercas — circular por el Río Guadiana
region_intro: Jogging around Villanueva De La Serena offers accessible routes through a fertile landscape in Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by the presence of the Guadiana River and its tributaries, which create wetlands and green corridors. The Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana provides traffic-free paths suitable for running, often alongside small rivers and streams. This environment supports diverse birdlife and offers a blend of natural scenery with historical points of interest. Best jogging routes around Villanueva De La Serena The most popular jogging route is Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular by the Río Guadiana, an 8.97 miles (14.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 35 minutes to complete. This route utilizes the Vía Verde, offering traffic-free paths along the Río Guadiana, ideal for birdwatching. Another top favourite among local runners is Running loop from Villanueva de la Serena, a moderate 6.11 miles (9.8 km) path. This route provides a convenient loop around the town, showcasing the accessible nature of the local running network. Local runners also love the La Haba loop from Villanueva de la Serena, an 8.53 miles (13.7 km) trail leading through the surrounding countryside, often completed in about 1 hour 26 minutes. Jogging around Villanueva De La Serena is defined by the Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana, the Guadiana River, and fertile agricultural landscapes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 200 runners have used komoot to explore Villanueva De La Serena's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available around Villanueva de la Serena?
A: There are over 25 running routes around Villanueva de la Serena, offering a variety of options for different preferences and fitness levels. These include easy, moderate, and more challenging trails.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on jogging routes in Villanueva de la Serena?
A: Jogging routes in Villanueva de la Serena primarily feature a fertile landscape, often crisscrossed by small rivers and streams. You'll find traffic-free paths, particularly along the &lt;strong&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/strong&gt;, and agricultural areas. The terrain is generally accessible and conducive to an active lifestyle.

Q: Are there different difficulty levels for running trails in the region?
A: Yes, the running network around Villanueva de la Serena offers routes for various abilities. You can find 3 easy routes, 17 moderate routes, and 8 more difficult trails, ensuring there's something for everyone from beginners to experienced runners.

Q: Can I combine my run with sightseeing or exploring historical sites?
A: Absolutely! The region blends natural scenery with historical points of interest. You can explore sites like the prehistoric &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt; or the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;, which offers breathtaking views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813279" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Guadiana River Viaduct&lt;/a&gt; also provides scenic views and pedestrian-friendly crossings.

Q: Are there opportunities for birdwatching while running?
A: Yes, the fertile landscape and wetlands created by the Guadiana River and its tributaries are perfect for birdwatching. Along routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282810779/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, you might spot diverse bird species such as storks, egrets, bee-eaters, plovers, and ibises. There's even a dedicated &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1813308" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birdwatching viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there family-friendly running paths in Villanueva de la Serena?
A: Many of the routes, especially those along the Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana, are traffic-free and relatively flat, making them suitable for families. These paths offer a safe and enjoyable environment for all ages to run or walk together.

Q: Are there dog-friendly running routes in the area?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations aren't always explicit, many of the natural trails and greenways, particularly the Vía Verde, are generally suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you follow local regulations regarding pet waste.

Q: What are some scenic running routes in Villanueva de la Serena?
A: For scenic runs, consider routes that follow the Guadiana River or utilize the Vía Verde. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282810779/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular by the Río Guadiana&lt;/a&gt; offers beautiful views of the river and opportunities for birdwatching. Routes leading to historical sites like Magacela Castle also provide picturesque backdrops.

Q: Where can I find easy running routes for beginners?
A: Beginners can find several easy routes, often characterized by minimal elevation gain and well-maintained paths. Look for routes that utilize the Vía Verde or stay closer to the town, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40314263" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Villanueva de la Serena loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate but accessible 6.87 km path.

Q: Are there circular running routes available?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1282810779/via-verde-vegas-del-guadiana-to-las-villuercas-circular-by-the-rio-guadiana" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Verde Vegas del Guadiana to Las Villuercas — circular by the Río Guadiana&lt;/a&gt;, an 8.97-mile trail. Another local favorite is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31848026" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Haba loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt;, which is 8.53 miles long.

Q: How long are the running routes typically around Villanueva de la Serena?
A: The running routes vary in length. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31861405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Masegales&lt;/a&gt; is about 7 km, while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20130552" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Villanueva de la Serena&lt;/a&gt; is around 9.8 km. Longer options like the Vía Verde routes can extend up to 14.4 km, offering choices for different training goals.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Villanueva de la Serena?
A: The region's climate suggests that spring and autumn are ideal for running, offering milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot in Extremadura, so early mornings or late evenings would be preferable during those months. Winters are generally mild, making running possible year-round, though some days might be cooler.
tips: A beautiful and quiet path that runs along the banks of the Río Guadiana where you can see its waters always on one side.
Bridge that allows us to cross to the other side of the Río Guadiana and that is pedestrian. It's worth getting there! It is part of the greenway that goes to Villanueva de la Serena.
